commit.message:
Fix dimension portal falling + item model parent for creative names

Dimension portal: Force-load target chunk before querying heightmap,
and fall back to y=100 if heightmap returns void. Prevents players
from spawning at y=-64 and falling to death in new dimensions.

Item models: Use itemSpec.itemType to infer handheld parent for
swords/tools with creative names (e.g. "Blade of Chaos" which
doesn't end in _sword). Previously only ID suffix patterns were
checked, causing all creatively-named tools to render as flat items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: skip Round 2 when Round 1 scores 100/100

Backend: break out of the rounds loop immediately after any round achieves
a perfect score (>=100) — no point tailoring again.
Frontend: Round 2 card shows 'Skipped — perfect score' with a muted style
instead of staying on 'Waiting...' when the backend skips it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

anti-spam. Añadir validación local de campos.

commit.message:
Force pick/pack-only during OT, raise picked_backlog penalty

During OT with open orders, mask out all tasks except pick and pack.
Every available minute goes toward throughput — no restock, no side
project, no idle.

Raised picked_backlog penalty from -0.5 to -2.0 per 10 orders so Jack
feels the cost of a stalled pack line before OT even starts.

commit.message:
fix: tool1 raises on OpenAI failure instead of returning silent error dict

Previously extract_keywords() swallowed exceptions and returned error strings
as keywords — causing '0 keywords extracted' and the pipeline continuing with
garbage data. Now raises RuntimeError so the job fails visibly in the UI with
the actual error (e.g. 'Incorrect API key') rather than silently proceeding.

commit.message:
fix: SuperTrend funding gate + TP drift adjustment + flip window

1. SuperTrend: add funding rate gate (±0.03% symmetric)
   Was the ONLY agent without funding check — multi-day holds
   could leak 1.5%+ to funding on extreme rates.

2. Executor: adjust TP to maintain R:R from actual fill price
   Signal TP based on 4h candle open, but fill at current price.
   Now recalculates TP preserving original R:R ratio from fill.
   Applies to ALL agents (harmless for trail strategies since
   TP stays unreachable).

3. Monitor: widen ST flip check window 10min → 20min
   Covers process restart edge case near 4h boundary.

Add null guards across materializer to prevent crashes from malformed specs

Fixes TypeError crashes when spec fields are undefined/missing due to
planner field name mismatches. Guards added to: fabric-scaffold (armor
set bonuses, loot injections, per-piece armor effects, brewing recipes),
advancement-generator (triggerEntityType, triggerBlockId), asset-mapping
(advancement title fallback), recipe-unlock-generator (triggerItemId,
recipeId), villager-trade-generator (profession, level, cost/result
items). Also fixes post-generation validator false positives for
family-derived block/item assets and vehicle/NPC naming conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add: weekly hold performance engine — computes returns for any bias source

weeklyHoldEngine.ts reads from canonical DB tables (pair_period_returns,
cot_snapshots, sentiment_aggregates) to compute weekly hold performance
for any bias source: Dealer, Commercial, Sentiment, Tiered V3,
2-of-3 Agreement, or Tandem.

No new crons, no new tables. READ-ONLY computation from existing data.
Supports single-week and multi-week aggregation with drawdown tracking,
per-asset-class breakdown, and trade-level detail.

[K8s] Support ephemeral-storage resource requests/limits on pods (#9053)

* [K8s] Support ephemeral-storage resource requests/limits on pods

Add ephemeral_storage as a first-class field in the resources task YAML,
allowing users to set ephemeral-storage requests on Kubernetes pods.
When set_pod_resource_limits is configured, limits are also set using
the same multiplier logic as CPU/memory. Validates that the field is
only used with Kubernetes.

* Fix undefined label in docs for ephemeral_storage

* Regenerate reference docs and fix formatting

* Improve ephemeral_storage docs based on review feedback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Modernize type hints and add pyright baseline (#3173)

* Modernize type hints to use builtin types and PEP 604 union syntax

Add ruff UP006/UP007/UP035 rules and auto-fix across the codebase:
List→list, Dict→dict, Tuple→tuple, Optional[X]→X|None,
Union[X,Y]→X|Y, and deprecated typing imports→collections.abc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add .git-blame-ignore-revs for typehint modernization commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fixes precommit

* Fixes leaf.py issue

* Add pyright type checking baseline with ratchet approach

Configure pyright in basic mode as a pre-commit hook, suppressing the
17 error categories that currently have violations (~830 errors). All
other basic-mode rules are active to prevent regressions. Future PRs
can enable suppressed categories one at a time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move pyright from pre-commit hook to CI workflow

Pre-commit CI doesn't have uv or project dependencies available.
Run pyright as its own GitHub Actions job instead, using uv sync
to install dependencies so pyright can see all type stubs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix pyright CI: use uvx to install and run pyright

pyright is not a project dependency, so uv run can't find it.
Use uvx (uv tool run) to download and run it ephemerally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Modernize type hints in DNLP files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace typing.List with list in psd.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 7 type errors across 4 pyright categories and enable them

Enable reportUndefinedVariable, reportOptionalIterable,
reportOptionalCall, and reportInvalidTypeArguments by fixing
the underlying issues rather than suppressing them. Also update
error count comments for remaining disabled categories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace bare-reference import pattern with noqa: F401 in import_solver

Standardize all import_solver methods to use `# noqa: F401` instead of
the bare-reference pattern (`import foo; foo`) for import-only checks.
Also enable reportUnusedExpression in pyright and remove remaining
disabled category lines that were fixed in the previous commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: risk-based position sizing across ALL 11 remaining agents

Every agent now uses: position_size = (equity × 0.5%) / sl_pct
instead of flat: position_size = equity × max_position_pct

Agents fixed: momentum, ema_ribbon, regime_momentum, mamc,
derivatives, breakout_retest, lvd, vcb, funding_decay,
supertrend, smc

Also: removed duplicate return None in 9 agents (_build_signal).

funding_decay uses 0.25% risk (half — counter-trend strategy).
supertrend/smc adapted for their unique variable names.

commit.message:
Add post-generation static validator to catch load-time and runtime crashes

New validator runs after materialization but before Gradle build. It checks
22 categories of issues across the generated file map without needing a
running Minecraft instance:

- Entrypoint integrity (fabric.mod.json ↔ Java classes)
- Mixin integrity (mixins.json ↔ mixin Java files)
- JSON validity sweep (all resource JSONs must parse)
- Block asset completeness (blockstate, model, texture per block)
- Item asset completeness (model, texture per item)
- Language file completeness (every item/block/entity/effect)
- Mob/NPC/Vehicle entity integrity (class, renderer, model, attributes)
- Dimension integrity (type JSON, dimension JSON, portal class)
- Container/Machine/CraftingStation integrity (BlockEntity, ScreenHandler, Screen)
- Recipe integrity (valid JSON, existing references, singular folder)
- Advancement integrity (icon items exist)
- Loot table integrity (drop items exist)
- Status effect textures, enchantment JSONs, worldgen data
- Custom arrow entity classes
- Cross-reference: mod-internal Java imports resolve to existing files
- Creative tab coverage (items appear in main class registrations)

Errors (will crash) block the build. Warnings (broken UX) are logged.
Includes 12 unit tests covering all major check categories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 모바일 계약서 단계 건너뛰기 방어 — VIEW→PRESIGN→sign 순서 강제

- confirm: PRESIGN은 VIEW 확인 후에만 허용
- sign: VIEW + PRESIGN 모두 완료된 경우에만 서명 허용

feat(admin): implement Stitch design features — 38 items across 7 tabs

Analytics tab:
- Telemetry bar with real-time UTC timestamp and pulse indicator
- System Pulse card (replaces Health) with API key status
- Token Consumption card with input/output breakdown
- Vector Storage card with progress bar (replaces Ingestion)
- Live Agent Activity feed from GET /agent/history
- Export Report button (downloads JSON token stats)
- New Agent Task modal (POST /agent/run)

Settings tab (renamed "Model Configuration"):
- Temperature slider (0-1) with Precise/Creative labels
- Top-P slider with Narrow/Diverse labels
- Max Tokens slider with context window % indicator
- Tool Execution on/off toggle
- Knowledge Base selector dropdown (from collections)
- System Prompt editor with line/char counter + markdown badge
- Reset Defaults + Test Configuration (POST /selftest) buttons

Tools tab (renamed "Skills & Tools"):
- Visual plugin cards grid with status badges and tool counts
- Performance metrics footer (Token Cost 24h, Total Calls, Models Active)

Vector DB tab (renamed "Knowledge Base"):
- Health status badge (Optimal/Degraded)
- Metrics cards (Total Chunks, Collections, Documents)
- Drag-and-drop upload zone with format badges (PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD)
- Select Files button with file input
- Indexing pipeline visualization (UPLOAD→CHUNK→EMBED→STORE→SYNC)
- Documents table with status badges, pagination, delete button
- Search filter by file type

Logs tab (renamed "System Logs"):
- Log level filter buttons (ALL/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/DEBUG)
- Client-side status code filtering
- Download logs button (JSON export)
- Connection status badge with Qdrant collection count
- Filtered/total count display

Chat tab:
- Active Model card with system uptime status
- Clear Session button
- Formatting toolbar (Bold, Code) above input

Plan document:
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-admin-ui-stitch-features.md

commit.message:
fix: decrypt OpenAI API key before passing to tailor crew

Keys are stored encrypted in DB. The tailor router was reading the raw
ciphertext and passing it to OpenAI, causing authentication errors and
score=0. Now calls decrypt() like every other router that uses credentials.

feat(combat): legendary action counter tracking (#101)

- Add cost? to CreatureAbility, legendaryActionCount to MonsterTemplate/Monster/CombatantState
- Backfill legendaryActionCount: 3 and cost: 1 for SRD monsters (Aboleth, Vampire)
- Add legendaryActionCount to monster upload validator
- Add useLegendaryAction and resetLegendaryActions pure functions to combat utils
- Reset legendaryActionsRemaining on turn advance in nextTurn (both branches)
- Render ⚡ R/N badge in combatant row (amber, data-testid)
- Add pool editor [−]/[+], per-action Use buttons, Restore All in detail panel
- Unit tests for new pure functions; E2E tests for full legendary action flow

* fix(combat): address PR review comments and improve test coverage

- Harden useLegendaryAction/resetLegendaryActions against negative/non-finite inputs
- Add validation for legendaryActionCount in validateMonsterData (non-negative integer)
- Refactor nextTurn to eliminate duplicated legendary reset logic
- Remove ?? 0 from legendaryActionsRemaining init (propagate undefined correctly)
- Pool editor now always visible for legendary combatants; decrement clamps remaining
- Increment pool preserves remaining (no implicit restore)
- Add data-testid to detail toggle button; fix brittle E2E SVG selector
- Normalize SRD Vampire Bite action name (remove cost from name, use cost field)
- Add unit tests for edge cases in useLegendaryAction and resetLegendaryActions
- Add integration tests for legendaryActionCount passthrough and validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(combat): improve diff coverage by extracting LegendaryActionsPanel component

- Extract legendary actions UI from page.tsx IIFE into LegendaryActionsPanel
  component in lib/components/ (no Firebase/Next.js deps — fully unit-testable)
- Add resetIncomingLegendaryPool, decrementLegendaryPool, incrementLegendaryPool
  pure utility functions to lib/utils/combat.ts
- Use resetIncomingLegendaryPool in nextTurn for cleaner code
- Add 17 unit tests for new utility functions
- Add 12 jsdom component tests for LegendaryActionsPanel (100% coverage)
- Extend jest testMatch to include .tsx test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(combat): refactor LegendaryActionsPanel tests to extract render helper

Extract renderPanel() helper to eliminate repeated await/act/render
patterns across 10 tests, reducing code clone count for Codacy gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(combat): address PR review feedback on legendary actions implementation

- Use String() instead of JSON.stringify() in validation error message
- Remove null bypass in legendaryActionCount validation guard
- Add proper type/finite/integer checks in transformMonsterData
- Replace brittle CSS selector with data-testid in E2E test
- Align spec with actual pool adjustment behavior (preserve/clamp)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(combat): harden legendary action helpers against non-finite inputs

- Add NaN/non-finite guards to decrementLegendaryPool and incrementLegendaryPool
- Normalize lacCount, lacRemaining, cost in LegendaryActionsPanel before use
- Add type="button" and aria-label to pool editor buttons
- Use canonical Tailwind classes (min-w-6, shrink-0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The pool editor buttons now have aria-label attributes for accessibility,
replacing the text-content-based accessible name used previously.
